items.title: A blunder in Narungga leaves SA's electoral authority in damage control

children.content: The 2026 state election has been a reputational disaster for South Australia's electoral authority. Its blunder in Narungga was the "cherry on top".

articles.title: Missing votes counted in key SA seat see One Nation margin increase

children.content: One Nation has retained the South Australian seat of Narungga, following a count of ballots that were missed on election day.

image.altText: One Nation's Chantelle Thomas at a media conference.

articles.title: Uncovered ballots prompt new count in key SA seat won by One Nation

children.content: The Electoral Commission of South Australia has uncovered more than 600 ballot papers that were not counted in the recent state election, including dozens from the closely contested seat of Narungga on the Yorke Peninsula.

image.altText: A composite image of two women with blonde hair

gallery.altText: A hand puts a lower house vote into a sealed ballot box as another person points to the top of the box

thumbnail.altText: A hand puts a lower house vote into a sealed ballot box as another person points to the top of the box

items.title: 'High-octane world' of drifting told through music in new concerto

children.content: Motorsport and orchestral music are coming together in a premiere in Adelaide this weekend.

image.altText: A clarinetist rehearsing a concerto.

items.title: Fishers deploy 'ninja tactics' to outsmart fish-stealing seals

children.content: Commercial fishers question their future as they compete with ravenous long-nosed seals that continue to rip man-sized holes in fish nets in South Australia's Coorong.

image.altText: A close up of the face of a wet seal resting on a log

items.title: Bomb squad removes 'volatile' explosives from Adelaide home, police say

children.content: Police say the bomb squad has now safely removed chemicals from the suburban Adelaide home of a 55-year-old man who has been charged with explosives offences, but say the matter is not terror-related.

image.altText: The South Australia Police logo on a building.

items.title: Bandicoots bounce back in Brown Hill Creek as team works towards 'superhighway'

children.content: A small colony of endangered brown bandicoots just south of Adelaide continue to breed as conservationists hope to connect them with other populations.

image.altText: Southern brown bandicoot, Isoodon obesulus obesulus

items.title: BreastScreen SA did not get ethics approval or consent before research, ombudsman finds

children.content: South Australia's public breast cancer screening service conducted research without ethics approval or consent from almost 100 patients, who it asked to do a second breast needle biopsy, prompting the service to "unreservedly" apologise.

gallery.altText: The office of BreastScreen SA in Adelaide.

thumbnail.altText: The office of BreastScreen SA in Adelaide.

items.title: Airport workers angered by surprise Qantas route cuts

children.content: Qantas's decision to cut one regional South Australian route and suspend four others came as a shock to workers at regional airports.

image.altText: A QantasLink plane on the tarmac

items.title: 'Attention-seeking' man allegedly targeted police, defence in 'cybercrime spree'

children.content: A 22-year-old Adelaide man who allegedly went on a "cybercrime spree", targeting several government departments and a courthouse, will remain behind bars until at the least the end of the year.

image.altText: Close up image of the face of a man with Asian heritage

items.title: 'No-one's committed murder here': Port CEO urges change to tribunal process

children.content: Port Adelaide's chief executive has slammed the AFL Tribunal process, labelling it "too legalistic" and "too adversarial".

image.altText: A man is a suit is frowning as he looks toward his left side.

items.title: SA government has 'assurances' from LIV Golf as course upgrade under fresh scrutiny

children.content: The South Australian government has reaffirmed its commitment to a $45 million upgrade of the North Adelaide public golf course to host LIV Golf in 2028, and has "assurances" about the tour's future.

image.altText: Two flags on a golf course with large buildings from the city in the background.

items.title: Australia's rarest reptile found after being considered extinct

children.content: Not found anywhere else on Earth, a skink considered functionally extinct has been found in a national park in far west NSW.

image.altText: A skink on a large rock

items.title: SA government apologises to prisoner denied abortion medication

children.content: The South Australian ombudsman calls for changes to medical monitoring at the Adelaide Women's Prison after a female prisoner was denied a medical abortion, which the health minister says is unacceptable.

image.altText: A building surrounded by a fence and trees

items.title: As Trump promotes a new ceasefire, many in Lebanon fear a bad deal

children.content: Donald Trump sold a ceasefire in Lebanon as a step towards peace. But that promise hinges on a condition that Lebanon may not be able to deliver.

gallery.altText: A displaced man gestures lying over belongings on a mini pickup, in Qasmiyeh Lebanon as he returns his family to their village
A displaced man gestures lying over belongings on a mini pickup, in Qasmiyeh Lebanon as he returns his family to their village

items.title: Outcry over 'disturbing' video of off-duty police officer pinning 13yo girl on train

children.content: Video of an off-duty police officer restraining a 13yo girl on a Perth train has sparked accusations of excessive force.

image.altText: A young girl holds her head and winces with her face being blurred.

items.title: Australia enters drone warfare era with Ghost Bat and Ghost Shark

children.content: Australia is on a push to keep pace with the rapid evolution of drone warfare, with an emphasis on locally produced Ghost Bat and Ghost Shark systems.

image.altText: A black mini submarine looking drone seen underwater.

items.title: The Windsor playbook with a twist: Harry and Meghan's royal model

children.content: Mixing philanthropy, influencing and brand building, Harry and Meghan’s Australian tour revives a question Queen Elizabeth once shut down: can the 'half in, half out' royal life work?

items.title: Turbulence ahead as airfares fluctuate amid Middle East conflict

children.content: Australians are finding domestic flights for as little as $65 on some routes, while others are paying thousands to travel interstate as airlines quietly reshape the market in response to conflict in the Middle East.

image.altText: The undercarriage of a jet plane as it takes off.

items.title: Viva Energy says fire at Victorian refinery will not cause fuel supply issues

children.content: Despite the refinery running at reduced capacity following a fire, experts say the incident in Geelong this week should not significantly affect fuel prices.

image.altText: An oil refinery

items.title: Chinese device found in strategic waters near Bali identified

children.content: A large torpedo-shaped device discovered near an island in a strategically important strait between Bali and Lombok has been identified by defence analysts as a Chinese undersea monitoring system.

gallery.altText: A torpedo-like object in the shallows at a beach.

thumbnail.altText: A torpedo-like object in the shallows at a beach.

items.title: Labor considering ways to spare new homes from capital gains changes

children.content: The possible budget centrepiece is likely to be finalised much closer to the May deadline than usual this year given global uncertainty, but progress has been made in recent weeks.

image.altText: A man in suit and tie holds his hands up in front of him as he speaks seriously.

items.title: The 'immense complexity' facing Ben Roberts-Smith's prosecutors

children.content: A legal expert says the criminal prosecution of former soldier Ben Roberts-Smith will test Australia's war crimes legal framework in ways never seen before, as the case moves toward a trial.

image.altText: Up close of Ben Roberts-Smith

items.title: Popular outback motoring rally cancelled due to fuel crisis

children.content: As fuel becomes less available, Variety NSW is concerned its main fundraising event could harm the small communities it tries to support.

image.altText: A car decorated like Thomas the Tank Engine driving through the outback

items.title: ABC 50/50 quiz: Test your knowledge of the Big Day Out, footy fairytales and the circles of hell

children.content: Every question has only two options: one is right, and the other is wrong. You have an even chance of guessing correctly each time.

image.altText: The three members of Nirvana pose for a photo.

items.title: It's generational warfare as millennials take on gen Z in Beef season 2

children.content: The dark comedy series returns with a biting look at generational conflict, starring Carey Mulligan and Oscar Isaac as a "combustible" middle-aged couple.

children.content: Our critics' picks for your weekend entertainment from the small screen to the kitchen oven.
